title: DoubleLine’s Jeffrey Gundlach Is Preparing for an Extreme Debt Scenario
summary: DoubleLine Capital’s Jeffrey Gundlach thinks the US government might restructure its debt.

title: Intesa Sanpaolo Beats on Profit as Trading Revenue Surges
summary: Intesa Sanpaolo SpA‘s first-quarter results came in better than expected on the back of a jump in trading revenue.

title: Shares of India’s Top Lender Slump on Interest-Income Miss
summary: State Bank of India’s shares dropped sharply, posting their biggest decline in nearly two years, after the nation’s top lender by assets reported weaker-than-expected net interest income.

title: US Stocks Rise as Tech Outlook Offsets War Worries: Markets Wrap
summary: A rally in technology stocks is lifting US index futures as investors wait for the monthly jobs report. Oil fluctuated while the Trump administration pushed for a peace deal with Iran.

title: Trump’s Latest 10% Global Tariffs Ruled Unlawful by US Trade Court
summary: US President Donald Trump’s 10% global tariffs were declared unlawful by a federal trade court just months after the US Supreme Court vacated earlier levies he’d imposed. Brendan Murray has the details on Bloomberg Television. (Source: Bloomberg)

title: Gold Rises as Buying Interest Counters Concern Over Hormuz Clash
summary: Gold rose, as signs of buying interest emerged even as fresh clashes in the Middle East threatened to fracture a fragile ceasefire.

title: Iran Reportedly Seized Tanker After Clash With US Near Hormuz
summary: The US and Iran clashed near the Strait of Hormuz, an escalation that threatens to further fracture a fragile ceasefire as the two sides discuss a permanent end to the war.

title: German Industrial Output Unexpectedly Falls for Second Month
summary: German industrial production unexpectedly fell in March for a second month, adding to worries for Europe’s largest economy as it grapples with the Iran war.

title: Pakistan Plans Debut Panda Bond Sale of Up to 1.75 Billion Yuan
summary: Pakistan is looking to raise yuan-denominated bonds in China’s onshore market, in what would mark its debut panda bond issuance, with a size of as much as 1.75 billion yuan ($257 million).

title: Ex-Hedge Fund Adviser Bets on AI-Backed Consultancy for Rich
summary: When Tony Cowell retired in 2023 from a three-decade career advising private equity and hedge funds from the Cayman Islands at KPMG LLP, AI was still relatively nascent.

title: Food Prices Rise to Highest in Three Years on Iran War Costs
summary: Global food prices climbed to their highest level in more than three years as the Iran war disrupted supply chains, raising the prospect of larger bills for shoppers.

title: Clariant CEO: Demand Remains Solid Despite Hormuz Blockade
summary: Demand for chemical products remains high, even though the blockade of the Strait of Hormuz has complicated supply chains, says Conrad Keijzer, CEO of Swiss chemicals company Clariant. He spoke on "The Pulse with Francine Lacqua" on Bloomberg TV.  (Source: Bloomberg)

title: UK Bonds Rally as Starmer Says He’ll Stay as Prime Minister
summary: UK government bonds rallied as Prime Minister Keir Starmer said he would stay as the Labour leader despite the party’s election losses.

title: BofA Didn’t Have Block Trade Mandate at Time of Segantii Calls
summary: Bank of America Corp. didn’t have a mandate to work on a block trade in Esprit Holdings Ltd. when it had phone calls with Segantii Capital Management to discuss the deal, a prosecution witness said during the hedge fund’s trial for insider trading.

title: Gold Fields’ Spat With Ghana Contractor Heads for Arbitration
summary: A $740 million dispute between Gold Fields Ltd. and a mining contractor in Ghana will be fought out in arbitration.

title: FSD Africa to Raise $400 Million SME Financing for Five Nations
summary: FSD Africa, an agency backed by the UK’s Foreign, Commonwealth & Development Office, is establishing funds to raise as much as $400 million of capital for small businesses in five nations.

title: BofA’s Hartnett Says US Stock Gains Have Rarely Been This Strong
summary: US stocks and gold are hurtling toward a fourth year of double-digit gains, rallies so exceptional that there have been few such episodes in history, according to Bank of America Corp. strategists.

title: Oil’s Price Surge Spurs Nigeria’s Flip From Discount to Darling
summary: Nigerian assets are rallying across stocks, bonds and the currency as investor confidence builds in President Bola Tinubu’s economic agenda.

title: Rheinmetall Loses Bull in JPMorgan After Slew of Results Misses
summary: Rheinmetall AG’s position as the standout European defense stock is looking increasingly vulnerable after a series of disappointing results led a long-time bull to downgrade the shares.

title: DBS Sells Private Credit Fund Yielding Up to 20% to Rich Clients
summary: Singapore-based firm Granite Asia has engaged the private banking arm of DBS Group Holding Ltd. to raise additional capital for its first private credit vehicle, people familiar with the matter said.

title: Investors Are Looking Through the Conflict, Goldman Sachs Says
summary: Christian Mueller-Glissmann, managing director for portfolio strategy and asset allocation at Goldman Sachs, discusses risk appetite amid US efforts to end the war in Iran. "A lot of the clients we speak to do very little in terms of taking a view on that conflict," Mueller-Glissmann tells Bloomberg Television. "They rather try to do tweaks and twists to the portfolio and focus on diversification." (Source: Bloomberg)

title: Rheinmetall CEO Sees High Mideast Demand for Air Defense Systems
summary: Rheinmetall AG sees growing demand for its air defense systems in the Middle East amid the Iran conflict and hopes to nearly double the number in use there by the end of 2027, the company’s chief executive officer said in an interview.

title: Stellantis Deal Gives China’s Leapmotor Plant, EV in Europe
summary: Stellantis NV is giving China’s Leapmotor access to a plant in Spain, and the partners will build an EV together to deepen ties as the maker of Opel and Fiat cars seeks to shore up its European operations.

title: Ghana Targets $1 Billion in Cocoa Bonds as Part of Overhaul
summary: Ghana is planning to raise $1 billion through domestic bonds to fund cocoa purchases from farmers, part of an overhaul of the way it delivers the commodity to global buyers.

title: Novo Nordisk’s Ozempic India Price Cut Boosts Obesity Drug Sales
summary: Novo Nordisk A/S saw a 40% surge in sales of its diabetes and weight-loss drugs in India last month, after steep price cuts boosted demand for its branded treatments even as generic GLP-1 therapies flood the market.

title: Mariana Mazzucato Thinks We Need More Moonshots | Odd Lots
summary: Today's guest Mariana Mazzucato is one of our most requested. Mazzucato, who is the director of the UCL Institute for Innovation and Public Purpose, specializes in the political economy of technological development and public sector investment. In our conversation, recorded in Madrid while we are at the Bloomberg CityLab conference, she explains her concept of the "mission economy," her definition of state capacity, how to prevent top talent from fleeing to the private sector, and whether consultants or governments should be blamed for inefficiencies and civic failures. It's a wide-ranging interview, one that covers everything from the initial public financing of Silicon Valley algorithms to the history of moonshots. (Source: Bloomberg)

title: Global Central Banks Boost Their Holdings of Malaysia Bonds
summary: Foreign central banks and governments have boosted their share of Malaysian sovereign bonds, underscoring the nation’s growing appeal as a reserve asset in a volatile global environment.

title: Bulgaria Lawmakers Approve Radev’s Cabinet After Landslide Win
summary: Bulgaria’s parliament voted in the nation’s first single-party government with a majority this century after Rumen Radev’s landslide victory, ushering in another European Union leader critical of the bloc’s mainstream.

title: South East Water CEO Quits After Pressure From UK Lawmakers
summary: The embattled chief executive officer of the UK’s South East Water resigned after a campaign by lawmakers and customers in the wake of multiple outages across Kent that affected tens of thousands of people.

title: M&G’s Fedeli Shifts to Cheaper Stocks After Tech’s Rapid Rally
summary: M&G Investment Management is taking profit in technology stocks that have seen rapid gains and is instead seeking undervalued companies with strong fundamentals given volatile markets, said executive Fabiana Fedeli.
